scope_boundary: Public-safe Project Theseus task-bundle summary import only. The import records pinned report hashes, 64 public BigCodeBench metadata-only tasks, 0 public training rows, 0 external inference calls, 0 task-level regressions, 18 benchmark gates, 19 residuals, visible artifact gaps, and expected-invalid overclaim controls from a dirty local source checkout. It does not rerun Project Theseus cleanly, reproduce benchmarks, export prompts/tests/solutions/traces/scores/candidate code, or validate model quality.
transition_reason: The bounded public task-bundle import is useful implementation-reference evidence because it preserves public metadata-only task counts, gate counts, residuals, artifact gaps, and non-claim boundaries while rejecting clean-live-replay, public-training leakage, prompt export, support-promotion, hidden-regression, hidden-gap, and failed-benchmark-gate controls. It remains a static summary import from a dirty checkout and therefore blocks promotion for clean live Theseus replay, model quality, benchmark superiority, generation speed, useful-solution-per-second, support-state, deployment, self-evolution, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: clean Project Theseus replay from a public-safe checkout or archived public release fixture
environment notes and exact replay command for the task-bundle lane
publication permission or redaction record for any public artifact bundle
missing-artifact closure or durable gap ledger with reviewer acceptance
negative_results: clean live Project Theseus replay remains unclaimed
source checkout was dirty at import time
public prompts, tests, solutions, traces, score labels, and candidate code are not copied into this repository
0 task-level regressions are recorded only inside the imported summary boundary
visible artifact gaps block support movement
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: public training rows or public prompt/test/solution/trace/score/candidate-code export accepted
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Project Theseus implementation-reference claim after clean public-safe replay or archived public fixture, environment notes, missing-artifact/publication-permission closure, verifier-quality review, benchmark-quality review where relevant, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: dirty local source checkout at import time
no exported prompts, tests, solutions, traces, scores, or candidate code
no clean live Project Theseus replay
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent Project Theseus, benchmark, model-quality, verifier-quality, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: dirty source checkout at import time
no publication-permission closure for broader artifacts
non_claims: does not promote Project Theseus above argument

does not prove clean live Project Theseus replay, deployed Theseus behavior, model quality, benchmark superiority, generation speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, support-state movement, or self-evolution safety
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the Project Theseus chapter core claim
